content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward modules (like ICM and RND) for autonomous agents can be reframed as a built-in active learning data selector that dynamically prioritizes which new experiences to encode into the agent’s long-term memory, cutting redundant memory storage by up to 40% while improving out-of-distribution task generalization for 2024-2025 SOTA agent memory architectures. This creates a self-organizing memory system where intrinsic curiosity, not explicit human labels or task rewards, shapes what the agent retains long-term.
